title: Integrate with rebase locally, merge for shared history
description: Rebase a private feature branch onto the latest base for a linear story, but use a merge when the branch is shared so no one's commits are rewritten.
body: Use when bringing a feature branch up to date with its base. Do not rebase a branch other people have already pulled. Actions: for a private branch, rebase onto the updated base to keep history linear and easy to review; for a shared branch, merge the base in so existing commit hashes are preserved; record which branches are shared. Validation signals include a review diff free of unrelated churn and teammates whose local branches still fast-forward cleanly. Risk boundary: rebasing published commits rewrites history others depend on and must be confirmed, never done silently.
